h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner expects the checklist to be the sole criterion for sign-off and asks what else is required.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Start with the unsafe condition: what latch behavior would stop the trial, even with every box ticked? Then put evidence on the bench: expected load, off-axis pull or bump response, and what failure would do to the user.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts the evidence standard from a checklist to an active physical test and safety judgment in response to the learner's reliance on the checklist.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square
scored.statedInsightEvidence: So “double the square” only works for the side, not the area, which is annoying because that was the evidence I was

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square
